behavior_summary: U8P_ENDLAMP::look is a simple examine/description handler for a lamp object. When the player 'looks at' or examines the lamp, the script emits a single short bark text 'lamp' and then ends/suspends the process.
No global flags are read or written, no menu/choice logic is present, and there are no external engine calls invoked by this script. The behavior is purely descriptive.
notable_control_flow: Direct linear flow: process entry -> emit bark 'lamp' -> suspend/terminate. No branching or loops.
Function runs as a process-type handler (U8P_ENDLAMP::look/examine); 'suspend' tokens in the raw indicate process lifecycle termination rather than additional logic.
open_questions: Are there any engine-level side effects (logging, analytics) tied to emitting a bark that are not visible in this script? (low confidence)
Does the object also receive other process handlers (use, touch) in other files that change behavior when looked at in different contexts? (low confidence)

main_function.name: U8P_ENDLAMP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines or 'looks at' the lamp object by emitting a single bark 'lamp'.
entry_conditions: Player examines or uses the 'U8P_ENDLAMP::look' action on the lamp object (engine invokes the U8P_ENDLAMP::look process).

function_map.name: U8P_ENDLAMP::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler that emits a single bark 'lamp'
